Security: Open source, 18 audits

Liquidity: High, TVL $2.2B

Security: Open source, 7 audits
Products: Lending Vaults, Borrow, Multiply
Highest APY on USDC: Vault (5.2%), Multiply (12%)

Security: 4 audits, not open source

Security: 11 audits, not open source

Security: Open Source, + Audited

Security: no info

Quick overview of $BP airdrop

Pre-market dumping to $260M FDV

MadLad holders got 1k tokens worth $260 at current price

And it's for OG NFT project of Solana

The airdrop basically covered fees you burned trading perps that's it

There no life-changing money

Low FDV is good for long-term price potential, but it makes the airdrop itself small

Staking utility:

- Up to +3% yield boost on USD
- Down to 0% maker fees
- Zero wire fees on deposits and withdrawals below $500K/30D
- Buy Backpack equity with tokens

First 7 days unstake anytime

After that 7-day unstake window

What are your thoughts? Let's discuss👇

tweets.full_text:
I find it funny that some projects claim they hate using influencers to push their products, yet they run straight to the Solana Foundation begging for the exact same kind of exposure influencers provide.

Of course, when Solana doesn’t see the ROI or value in the project, ignores them, or refuses to highlight them, they start crying, complaining, and blaming the Solana Foundation for everything.

Solana’s social media marketing through the main Solana accounts works in the same way influencer accounts do lmao. They may not directly charge money for tweets, but they choose to support projects that benefit them in the long run. While the Solana Foundation is structured as a non-profit, there are still economic incentives aligned with the growth and success of the Solana ecosystem.

The reality is that the engagement a project can get from a few strong Solana creators can easily match or even surpass the engagement from the main Solana account.

Some people will say, “yeah, but Solana’s reputation is better, and they don’t promote scams.” I get the point, but it's just not true anymore. There have been countless projects highlighted by the Solana account, founders, or team members that later turned out to be scams. I don’t even blame them btw. If you’ve been in this space long enough, you will inevitably believe in something that ends up rugging.

As more and more projects compete for Solana’s main account support or Toly’s retweets, I think projects should consider working with a few trustworthy, reputable Solana voices to push their protocols instead of focusing entirely on the Solana Foundation.

The Solana Foundation used to intervene very little in publicly supporting projects back in 2021, and they didn’t even have the engagement or attention they have now. Builders used to independently build without relying on the foundation or blaming it for not gaining attention. We got here thanks to resilient OG builders who built with little SF intervention and no main account tweets.

I don’t know when projects became so entitled and dependent on SF support, but I don’t think it truly helps projects in the long run. If your project’s success depends on Solana’s main account support, I’m sorry, but you will probably fail.

My point is simple: exposure is exposure, and incentives are incentives. Whether it comes from a foundation, an exchange, or an influencer, the mechanics are the same. Let’s not pretend one channel is morally superior to another because tbh Solana influencers, Solana users, and the Solana Foundation are all incentivized by the same economic forces. If you focus on building something that actually creates value, third parties will naturally choose to talk about you.

result.text:
Need to address some glaring inaccuracies in the current discourse about @Solana.

Would bet a significant amount that we (at SF & as an ecosystem) do more to support founders/builders than any other network, and it’s probably not even that close.

First, let’s talk about funding:

- Since Jan 1, we’ve run 3 Hackathons (Privacy, Consumer/NFTs/Gaming, Agents), and Labs ran their own (Mobile), and there are many more coming including the global Solana hackathon with Colosseum. Additionally, eco teams like Ranger and Arcium recently debuted hackathons. These come with millions in prizes.

- Solana builders can join @colosseum or @incubator for YC-style funding, investments, and mentorship with our direct support. Founders in Colosseum alone have gone on to raise $650 million+ in venture funding.

- @Superteam is our global founder network, with thousands of members - and we just launched Superteam USA to fund and assist founders in the biggest market. Superteam is Solana family, designed for builders.

- Superteam offers instagrants up to $10k + Earn which has paid out millions, YC founders building on Solana can get up to $50k extra, Kalshi x Solana has a $2m fund for prediction markets, and SF offers evergreen grants for open source & public goods with a $40k average check size. Many other teams offer grant-style funding as well, including Monke Foundry, Metaplex, Wormhole, Bonk, Seeker, etc.

In total, Foundation and adjacent entities fund tens of millions collectively each year into early stage startups and builders who are advancing the network, with no equity commitments or tradeoffs - in service of growing the ecosystem.

Second, let’s talk about distribution:

- Foundation holds events all over the world directly, including Accelerate and Breakpoint, but also assists with funding and resources for community events like mtndao, Solana Summit, and many other hyper regional events. Our entire goal: put builders on stage, give them an audience, and then help them distribute their message.

- Since Jan 1, @Solana has amplified 300+ different ecosystem companies. In fact we handle so much inbound load from teams wanting amplification that we built multiple targeted handles to do this even better, like @capitalmarkets, @solanapayments, and @x402.

- Even in Gaming, we dedicate distribution resources to builders through @solanagaming, and stream games multiple times per week.

- Our distribution has real impact. Last month we sent our live team to @mtndao to host Demo Day, and platformed dozens of builders by putting them on stage, livestreaming them, and then clipping their segments. One team, Tapestry, noted that after the stream they had thousands of new downloads on their mobile app.

- @Luminaries, our top content creator collective, distributes stories from ecosystem builders through 50+ influencers across many different channels

- We produce hundreds of high quality videos and clips per year across stories, explainers, and education that feature our ecosystem teams

- SF hosts 10 regular podcasts to feature ecosystem teams, and sponsors and produces countless others, like The Index and Genfinity. In this case, we acquire media channels & time and we directly book guests from our ecosystem that have a story to tell

Last year, we led all crypto networks in total impressions and engagement on X & LinkedIn. Again, I would guess it wasn't really that close.

If you’re building on Solana, our firehose is yours to use.

Solana is not only the best place to build, it’s the best place to win customers and build a giant business. No network has produced more multi-billion $ companies with real, sustainable businesses and moats.

If we don’t know you yet and you’re here, we would love to hear from you and see how we can help. That’s quite literally our job.

Don't believe everything you read on X 🫡

tweets.full_text:
300 military showed up at our event.

During my stay at Founders Villa by @SuperteamAE, I met @cryptoslice. 

We connected over shared interests: meditation, workouts, breathing, health. 

Soon after, together with @taras_13, we had an idea: a wellness bootcamp for founders. 

That’s how @solana_temple was born. 

We applied for financial support, and @therealchaseeb from Solana Foundation believed in it. 

For the next 2.5 months, we focused fully on building the program. 

In the end: 
~ 105 participants 
~ Partners: @solanaidentity, @Backpack, @LimeChainHQ, @okx and more
~ Support from @solana 

Everything started perfectly: 
~ Daily wellness: yoga,breathing sessions, meditation, fitness, silent days. 
~ Workshops: fundraising, pitching, marketing, community. 

The vibe was unreal. Builders connecting, sharing, growing. 

Then… everything changed. 

At 7am, ~300 military personnel arrived at the resort. 

Staff couldn’t enter. Operations stopped. We had to clean, manage, and run everything ourselves. 

Some events were canceled. Tension grew. 

But we adapted: moved activities; changed locations; held Demo Day at another venue. 

One of the hardest and most exciting experiences I’ve had. 

Key lessons: 
~ If you believe in the idea - push it 
~ Healthy community = real support 
~ Always plan for risks

Have you had a moment when everything stopped going to plan?

Institutions are moving to @solana, but engineering complexity, compliance, and fragmented tooling have been real challenges even for those who’ve already taken the first step, with many others still looking for a clear way in.

That's why we're excited to announce that we're powering Solana Developer Platform (SDP): a single AI-ready API for institutions to issue, settle, and trade on-chain.

We've been building Solana infrastructure since before the testnet, serving the biggest DeFi and institutional players, and stress-testing it every day for the past six years. That same layer now underpins SDP, delivering:

- Compliant permissioned transaction routing through Yellowstone Shield
- Enterprise-grade bare-metal servers distributed globally across 3 continents
- Stake-weighted priority transaction sending through Jet
- Zero-gap real-time and historical data through our Yellowstone suite

@SolanaFndn just made it easy for every institution to build financial products on-chain. We're making sure the infrastructure is rock-solid (our customers say we’re the best at it).

Learn more 👇
https://t.co/cwdxJhMiNS

result.text:
Institutions are moving to @solana, but engineering complexity, compliance, and fragmented tooling have been real challenges even for those who’ve already taken the first step, with many others still looking for a clear way in.

That's why we're excited to announce that we're powering Solana Developer Platform (SDP): a single AI-ready API for institutions to issue, settle, and trade on-chain.

We've been building Solana infrastructure since before the testnet, serving the biggest DeFi and institutional players, and stress-testing it every day for the past six years. That same layer now underpins SDP, delivering:

- Compliant permissioned transaction routing through Yellowstone Shield
- Enterprise-grade bare-metal servers distributed globally across 3 continents
- Stake-weighted priority transaction sending through Jet
- Zero-gap real-time and historical data through our Yellowstone suite

@SolanaFndn just made it easy for every institution to build financial products on-chain. We're making sure the infrastructure is rock-solid (our customers say we’re the best at it).

Learn more 👇
https://t.co/cwdxJhMiNS

Weekly Update – @NOMADZxyz | Week 13 [2026]  

1.1 Quick wins: 
1. Launched events in a globe format 
2. Added bookings for multiple rooms and discount codes 
3. Designed and shipped Essential badges for loyal users 
4. Built a partnership with RadiantsDAO 
5. Crossed 17K registered users   

1.2 What's next: 
1. Platform: completed badges for multiple bookings, improve admin panel with more data, discounts, an aggregator v.2 
2. Mobile: Launch v. 10 with community verification, new badges and AI integration 
3. Nomadz 100: Cohort 2 launch campaign with 16 new creators, 

2. Highlights  
2.1 Product - Platform 
1. Improved search speed - faster results across stays, events, and nearby discovery. 
2. Added new upcoming events for Q2 2026 and continued expanding the Earth interface. 
3. Continued iterating on the referral dashboard based on user feedback.  

2.2 Product - Mobile 
1. Shipped release #10 to the Solana Mobile dApp Store: AI agent integration, new quests and badges. 
2. Release #11 in progress: Communities verification + new badges  

2.3 Growth
1. Recorded a podcast with @mango_ 
2. Were added as a travel partner to the Accelerate Miami website by @SolanaEvents
3. Sent the second email campaign: 15,000 emails delivered. 
4. Crossed 17,000+ registered users and 19,800+ followers on X. 
5. 4 weeks in a row updates provided to @DeltaV_xyz 
6. Collaborated with @sns and @SuperteamMY on the Solana Network State at @ns

 3. Lowlights  
1. We were not able to run the community campaign due to negotiations with a partner. Will launch it this week. 
2. We had a few users struggling to verify using Matrica Connect. We added a button to register.  

4. Core Metrics  
1. Total Users: 17,072 (↑ from 16,806 last week) 
2. Active Users (week): 834 (↑ from 977 last week) 
3. New Users (week):  266 (↑ from 250 last week) 
4. DAU: ~120 on average (↑ from 139 last week) 
5. Transactions on Solana (week): ~3425 (↑ from 905 last week, ~488/day) 
6. X Followers (@NOMADZxyz): 19,835+ (↑ from 19,764)  

5. This Week's Focus  
→ Launch aggregator update for platform and app on Solana dApp Store 
→ Polish badges, discount codes, events calendar with side events 
→ Scale email campaign to full 17K registered users 
→ Launch Nomadz 100 Cohort 2 and have the second community call 
→ New partnerships and launch campaigns with @cherrydotfun and @RadiantsDAO

 6. Asks  
1. Looking for content creators who are actively traveling to join Cohort 2 of Nomadz 100 - apply or refer someone  

7. Shoutouts 
Big thanks to the @solanamobile, @solana and @SolanaFndn teams for trust and support with growth. And to everyone who engaged with our content and used Nomadz.

tweets.full_text:
Network School - worth it or not?

In March 2025 I went to @balajis Network School (@ns).

First thing you need to know before going there {especially as a founder} is why it’s special.

For the price you pay you get everything covered:
• living
• cleaning
• food (based on @bryan_johnson)
• facilities (gym, ice bath, sauna, co working, content room etc.)
• workshops (10-20 weekly on crypto, robotics, AI etc.)

So you don’t spend time on typical stuff that distracts you, and it saves you a LOT of time that you can spend on:

~ networking
~ education
~ leveling up your startup
~ wellness + sport

Minimum time you can stay is 1 month, but because of the benefits I mentioned, this 1 month can generate more outcomes than 3 months of normal life.

For me, everything I was getting there was a must-have, so my plan to stay for just 1 month turned into 2 months.

During that time, together with @taras_13, @cryptokitty3x and @venture_launch, we also contributed to the life of NS residents. We hosted:

~ CUDIS Wellness Event
~ Solana Journey Event
~ Introduction Circle
~ Solana workshop
~ Solana Pong

Personal things that I am proud of:

• 55 days of workouts 
• 4 new books 
• 13 min in plank (won 2 monthly completions)
• won 2 poker tournaments 

One more important thing is that you can come up with new ideas for your startup and get valuable feedback from crypto people and experts in the space.

During my stay at NS we launched the beta of @NOMADZxyz and got strong feedback from early testers on site, which directly helped shape our next product iterations.

And the most important, I built relationships with Chris and Viktor who became my friends.

Final words:

This is an experience where all the benefits can’t be fully described in a post, thread or article.

You just have to experience it once and thank me later if this post is how you first heard about NS.

Have you been to NS before or do you plan to come sometime?

The first credit market denominated in the Brazilian Real on @solana is now live on Loopscale.

BRZ — @transferogroup's stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the Real — pairs with @etherfuse's TESOURO, tokenized Brazilian treasury bonds.

Deposit BRZ or TESOURO into the new TESOURO/BRZ Loop for leverage on Brazilian sovereign debt, with collateral and yield both denominated in the Real. 🇧🇷

BRZ lenders can also earn yield through the BRZ UM Vault, which lends against TESOURO.

tweets.full_text:
- No insiders
- No VCs
- No team tokens

Just users

Backpack TGE is tomorrow 🎒

Stake or dump? Let's find out:

1️⃣ Tokenomics

- 25% at TGE (24% points + 1% Mad Lads)
- 37.5% pre-IPO (unlocked by achievements)
- 37.5% post-IPO (fully locked until 1 year post-IPO)

The community will be the only one holding tokens for the first weeks, and new tokens only get "minted" when certain milestones are achieved.

What those milestones look like is unknown so far.

The whole idea is similar to MetaDAO's idea of unlocking team tokens at certain FDV thresholds — but still very different.

Pre-IPO tokens don't all go to the team, and it's more important for Backpack to meet company KPIs than to hit a certain FDV.

Team allocation goes to the "Corporate Treasury," locked until at least one year post-IPO. The team owns equity in the company, and the company owns a large share of the token supply.

2️⃣ TGE and Airdrop

As mentioned, the community will own 100% of the circulating supply.
Prediction markets currently suggest an FDV of $300M, which would imply an airdrop worth $75M and 1 point = $0.20.

1 Backpack point ≈ $0.20
1 Mad Lad ≈ $350*

*I doubt everyone will get the same allocation for Mad Lads tho.

Armani has always talked about rewarding loyalty — see a proposed model below.

Overall, short-term price action shouldn't be overvalued, especially in a bear market and with a project like Backpack. This could take months or years to reach the price it deserves.

3️⃣ IPO Model

While other exchanges like Binance, Crypto. com, and KuCoin mostly did ICOs or private sales with less than 15% initial supply, Backpack is going dual: token and IPO.

A dual model means profits are somehow split between token holders and equity holders.

That usually ends with either two weak assets — or one strong and one down bad.

Guess which one is normally down bad...

There's a risk we get a token that the team and investors don't actually want to hold.

And IPOs take years.

The upside? The token will likely trade at a premium pre-IPO.

A dual system (token + equity) has never worked before.
But honestly if anyone can pull it off, it's Backpack.

4️⃣ Numbers and future

- $400B+ cumulative trading volume

- $100M+ annual revenue in 2025

- $1B pre-money valuation (unicorn status)

- 150+ countries served

Future plans include:

- Tokenized stock trading (real ownership rights)
- Physical cards
- Checking and savings accounts
- Real-world assets (gold, oil, commodities)

5️⃣ Stake or dump?

Backpack announced that 20% of their equity will go to users who stake for 1 year — something no one has done before.

This gives the community a real chance to own a piece of the company. (Love this.)

But this market is tough, and I know a lot of farmers who've been waiting on this airdrop for months trying to offset losses. People need liquidity.

The price action isn't in Armani's hands, but I'm sure $BP will find its fair price over the years.

If you need the money — dump it. The price could definitely go lower.

If you don't, and you believe in Backpack's vision join the experiment and forget about what you staked. There's a solid chance you get rewarded in a few years.

Overall, you can complain that the whole seasons thingy went on way too long but Backpack is one of the teams in crypto with the highest moral compass. They tried to do things the right way, without creating unnecessary risk for themselves, the platform, or the community.

The community will be the only one holding tokens for the first weeks, and new tokens only get "minted" when certain milestones are achieved.

What those milestones look like is unknown so far.

The whole idea is similar to MetaDAO's idea of unlocking team tokens at certain FDV thresholds — but still very different.

Pre-IPO tokens don't all go to the team, and it's more important for Backpack to meet company KPIs than to hit a certain FDV.

Team allocation goes to the "Corporate Treasury," locked until at least one year post-IPO. The team owns equity in the company, and the company owns a large share of the token supply.

2️⃣ TGE and Airdrop

As mentioned, the community will own 100% of the circulating supply.
Prediction markets currently suggest an FDV of $300M, which would imply an airdrop worth $75M and 1 point = $0.20.

1 Backpack point ≈ $0.20
1 Mad Lad ≈ $350*

*I doubt everyone will get the same allocation for Mad Lads tho.

Armani has always talked about rewarding loyalty — see a proposed model below.

Overall, short-term price action shouldn't be overvalued, especially in a bear market and with a project like Backpack. This could take months or years to reach the price it deserves.

3️⃣ IPO Model

While other exchanges like Binance, Crypto. com, and KuCoin mostly did ICOs or private sales with less than 15% initial supply, Backpack is going dual: token and IPO.

A dual model means profits are somehow split between token holders and equity holders.

That usually ends with either two weak assets — or one strong and one down bad.

Guess which one is normally down bad...

There's a risk we get a token that the team and investors don't actually want to hold.

And IPOs take years.

The upside? The token will likely trade at a premium pre-IPO.

A dual system (token + equity) has never worked before.
But honestly if anyone can pull it off, it's Backpack.
